bio: I study how light, inorganic carbon, dissolved organic matter, and particles regulate mercury redox cycling in marine systems.

My work combines lab photochemistry, trace-metal clean sampling, continuous dissolved gaseous mercury measurements, and multi-compartment modeling to connect process-level kinetics with large-scale ocean behavior.
Across contaminated estuaries and ice-influenced Arctic waters, I focus on the ocean-atmosphere interface where mercury is transformed, exchanged, and redistributed.

focusAreas.title: Mechanistic Understanding of Marine Biogeochemical Processes
focusAreas.description: Elucidating the mechanistic controls of marine biogeochemical processes, particularly trace-element cycling, through process-based field observations integrated with experimental approaches.

focusAreas.title: Quantitative Parametrization and Predictive Modeling of Biogeochemical Cycles
focusAreas.description: Identifying key environmental parameters governing biogeochemical reactions and incorporating these relationships into model parametrizations to improve predictive understanding of global biogeochemical cycles and their response to climate change.

projects.title: Photoreduction mechanisms of dissolved divalent mercury in surface seawater
projects.outcome: Demonstrated enhancing roles of carbonate and thiols, and the suppressing role of aromatic DOM on Hg(II) photoreduction in seawater.

projects.title: Air-sea exchange, sediment methylation, and ecosystem sources of mercury in the Arctic Ocean
projects.support: Korean Institute of Marine Science & Technology (KIMST)
projects.outcome: Estimated air-sea Hg(0) exchange across sea-ice regimes and first linked photoredox kinetics to DOM composition in Arctic seawater.

projects.title: Investigation of Water Qualities and Sediment Contamination in Hyeongsan River Estuary
projects.outcome: Investigated the biogeochemical cycling of dissolved and particulate Hg in a contaminated estuary.

projects.title: Rainwater Hg monitoring as part of the Asia-Pacific Mercury Monitoring Network
projects.outcome: Tracked potential Hg sources in wet deposition.

skills.description: Designed photo-experimental setups for estimating photoredox reaction rate constants and developed continuous measurements of dissolved gaseous Hg(0) in surface waters.
Sampling techniques for trace elements in ambient water and sediment.
CVAFS workflows with BrooksRand and Tekran 2537 series for water, sediment, and atmospheric mercury analyses, including kinetic measurements.
Dissolved organic carbon by VarioTOC, solid phase extraction for FT-ICR-MS, and fluorescent dissolved organic matter analysis with Hitachi F-2500.
Chlorophyll-a fluorometry, suspended particulate matter, UV-Vis spectrophotometry, acid volatile sulfide, YSI meter, glove box operation, and ferrioxalate actinometry.
R, MATLAB, Excel, QGIS, Ocean Data View, SigmaPlot, SPSS, MINEQL+5.0, Mercury Guru, Hg Logger Plus, and multi-compartment Hg modeling.
